I’ve looked through your profile, and your experience matches an Insights Analyst role that I am currently hiring for and I believe you’d be a great fit!  Location : Hyderabad ( Hybrid)  Why we need you: Within Data and Analytics, the Analytics Solutions team is responsible for identifying and tracking business trends. We also innovate new ways of looking at company data or defining KPIs and provide data & analytics support and guidance to the rest of the business.  We are looking for a team member to be the first point of contact and analytical partner for our stakeholders. You will be reporting to the Senior Manager of Ecosystem Analytics, in a growing team of analysts and you will be working closely with data scientists and other members of Data and Analytics.  Key Responsibilities:  Be the analytical partner for stakeholders, informing them and discussing actions when required Provide regular or ad-hoc analyses, and deliver actionable insights, with ability to cater for different audiences  The ideal candidate will have: (1+ year of exp on SQL) Understanding of gaming business model Degree in quantitative area (Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, Computer Science) or relevant industry experience Experience with large datasets and analysis tools such as  Python, R, or equivalent (or a strong desire to learn) and fluency in SQL Experience in applying statistical techniques to problems (e.g. hypothesis testing, causal impact, regression analysis, time series analysis, or similar) Ability to present and explain results of your analyses  If you are interested in the role, please share your resume at shagufta_khan@flutterint.com and please mention your years of experience along with current and expected CTC.  Regards, Shagufta Khan Talent Acquisition Partner | Data and Automation | DEI Champion | Building Strong Talent Communities for Organizations

CONVERSATION TITLE: Start your Open Source AI Journey with OPEA & Intel Xeon
CONTENT:
Rethinking GenAI? You’re not alone.

More and more teams are moving away from proprietary platforms like OpenAI—and toward open, secure, and cost-effective alternatives.

The Open Platform for Enterprise AI (OPEA) makes that transition easier. Built on open standards and powered by Intel® Xeon®, it gives you the flexibility to fine-tune models, run inference locally, and stay in control of your data.

ISTE+ASCD recently made the switch—ditching OpenAI and building a private, high-performing RAG chatbot using OPEA and Intel® Xeon® processors. The result? Lower cost per user (by over 90%) and zero trade-offs on accuracy or speed.

If you're exploring how to scale GenAI while keeping your data secure and your infrastructure lean, OPEA is worth a closer look.

See how you can do more—with less.

Today, Companies can overcome these challenges and significantly enhance plant safety by implementing sensor based monitoring solutions for process-critical and non-process-critical auxiliary assets.


Download our complimentary eBook and see how you can take a smarter, faster and simpler approach to auxiliary asset monitoring.


What are operator rounds not telling you? Learn more about the key reasons behind your asset monitoring challenges.
